name: Dark Plugg
description: An underground micro-genre born from the collision of Atlanta plugg production and a self-conscious dark aesthetic — gothic atmosphere, horror-film textures, and deliberately cold vocals. Emerged almost entirely on SoundCloud between 2019 and 2021 with no label infrastructure, no press coverage, and no mainstream entry point. The artists involved built an insular scene through anonymous producer tags, SoundCloud reposts, and tight-knit collaborator networks. It sits downstream from Memphis phonk, Atlanta plugg, and the SoundCloud rage wave — and it feeds directly into the Opium label sound, digicore, and contemporary underground rap.

timeline.proto_period: 2017–2018: Plugg producers (DJ Smokey, DJ Yung Vamp, Richie Souf) begin stripping trap back to skeletal 808s and minor-key piano. Memphis phonk aesthetics start bleeding into Atlanta plugg.
timeline.emergence: 2019: osamason, Summrs, Autumn!, and BabySantana begin posting on SoundCloud. The sound has a name — dark plugg — within the community, though never officially coined.
timeline.peak_underground: 2020–2021: Cartier God, KanKan, and Izaya Tiji deepen the production language. Sematary introduces horror plug — a more extreme branch. The scene reaches ~50 active artists.
timeline.crossover_pressure: 2022: Yeat, Ken Carson, and Destroy Lonely pull elements of the aesthetic into Opium/mainstream adjacent rap. Dark plugg artists remain underground but gain visibility.
timeline.current_status: 2024: Expanding — core artists still operating underground, second wave forming. Cash Cobain and NYC sample drill begin pulling adjacent energy toward a more melodic, sample-based offshoot.

lineage_roots.name: Plugg
lineage_roots.description: Atlanta production style developed around 2012–2016: melodic 808s, minor keys, slow tempos, and a heavy sedated atmosphere. Key producers: Richie Souf, Southside, Pi'erre Bourne. Plugg gave dark plugg its sonic skeleton.

lineage_roots.name: Memphis Phonk
lineage_roots.description: 1990s Memphis rap (Three 6 Mafia, Lil Ugly Mane, DJ Screw lineage) contributed the horror-adjacent dark atmosphere, lo-fi cassette texture, and esoteric occult energy that distinguishes dark plugg from regular plugg.

lineage_roots.name: SoundCloud Rap / Rage
lineage_roots.description: The 2017–2018 SoundCloud wave (Lil Pump, XXXTENTACION, Playboi Carti) established the platform infrastructure and distorted 808 palette. Rage production — heavy 808s, pitched-down samples — fed directly into dark plugg's sonic approach.

lineage_roots.name: Drain Gang / Bladee
lineage_roots.description: Stockholm's Drain Gang (Bladee, Ecco2k, Thaiboy Digital, Yung Lean) provided a parallel example of making dark, esoteric, internet-native music outside the industry. Dark plugg artists absorbed the ethos of anonymity and aesthetic-over-commercial.

key_artists.name: osamason
key_artists.notes: Widely credited as the artist who gave the sub-genre its emotional core. osamason's melodic introspection and deliberate lo-fi aesthetic set the template others followed.

key_artists.name: Summrs
key_artists.notes: Developed the production language connecting plugg to rage. Prolific output across 2020–2022 helped define the scene's tempo, tone, and vocal style.

key_artists.name: Autumn!
key_artists.notes: One of the purest examples of the dark plugg sound — skeletal beats, sparse delivery, cold atmosphere. Collaborates heavily within the tight-knit network.

key_artists.name: BabySantana
key_artists.notes: Embodies the second-wave dark plugg artist — emerged directly from listening to osamason and Summrs, represents the self-replicating nature of the scene.

key_artists.name: Cartier God
key_artists.notes: One of the key producers who codified the dark plugg production sound — sparse kick patterns, minor-key piano stabs, deliberately cheap-sounding 808s that read as aesthetic choice not limitation.

key_artists.name: Izaya Tiji
key_artists.notes: Connects the osamason/dark plugg world to the Ken Carson / Opium adjacency. Sits at the seam between fully underground and mainstream-facing.

key_artists.name: KanKan
key_artists.notes: Production-focused artist operating at the deepest end of the sub-genre. Shares production credits with Cartier God.

key_artists.name: Sematary
key_artists.notes: Founded the horror plug offshoot — a maximally dark branch using horror film samples, blown-out distortion, and death imagery. Haunt Network collective.

sound_characteristics: Vocal auto-tune used for texture not correction

key_producers.name: DJ Smokey
key_producers.contribution: Established the skeletal 808 / minor melody template that dark plugg built on

key_producers.name: DJ Yung Vamp
key_producers.contribution: The deconstructed, near-silent plugg beats that sound like they're falling apart

key_producers.name: Richie Souf
key_producers.contribution: Trap-to-plugg production bridge; worked with Playboi Carti early on

key_producers.name: Soza
key_producers.contribution: Contemporary dark plugg production; shared pipeline with Cartier God

key_producers.name: Cartier God
key_producers.contribution: Codified the 2019-2021 dark plugg production language

connections_to_mainstream.connection: Carti's Whole Lotta Red (2020) absorbed plugg/rage aesthetics and pushed them into mainstream visibility. Dark plugg artists exist in his shadow but reject the commercial framing.
Carson and Destroy Lonely (Opium label) pulled the plugg aesthetic into the mainstream streaming era. Several dark plugg artists have collaborated or been compared to the Opium sound.
Yeat's commercial breakthrough brought plugg energy to wide audiences. His rise coincided with dark plugg peak underground moment — parallel evolution, different trajectory.

why_it_matters: Dark Plugg is a rare example of a sub-genre that emerged, developed a distinct identity, and sustained a community entirely outside industry infrastructure. No blogs covered it during its formation. No labels signed it. The scene policed its own aesthetic through SoundCloud comments, reposts, and producer tags. It is a case study in how contemporary underground music forms: not in cities, not in venues, but in algorithmic recommendation chains on streaming platforms and anonymous comment sections.
